highlights.text: As I discussed yesterday though (and as anyone who has ever traded a downtrend or bear market in ES knows), f or every “elevator down” move in ES, there is a correspondent short squeeze .
This concept would be key for today’s CPI day as well as we saw another “elevator down” move, followed by short squeeze .

Some days, they are very clean and one directional, but most often the first move, and often the second and third are traps.It is for this reason that I prefer to trade failed breakdowns on CPI days…..Wait for a level to flush, run stops, and reclaim before entering.” We saw this today, with an initial spike down to 5472, rally to 5505, followed by a monster sell down to 5414, losing yesterdays 5448 lows.
I will also discuss an important variation of my core setup: The Failed Breakdown.

post_title: Massive Bear Trap For SPX, Is A Bottom In? September 12 Plan
